---
title: "Poster Guidelines"
description: "Producing conference posters with Rashid Lab branding"
---

This page covers academic poster production using the lab's
`--rl-*` token system. See [Color Palette, Typography & CSS Tokens](colors.qmd)
for the canonical token reference.

::: {.callout-note}
## New in April 2026

A poster template was missing from the lab brand assets prior to April
2026. This page introduces a starter Quarto Typst template that produces
print-quality posters with the same visual register as
[naimurashid.github.io](https://naimurashid.github.io). The template
lives at `assets/branding/poster-template.qmd`.
:::

## Standard sizes

Most conferences accept either format. Confirm with the organizers before
printing.

| Format | Dimensions (inches) | Dimensions (cm) | Use |
|--------|--------------------|-----------------|-----|
| Landscape | 48 × 36 | 122 × 91 | ASA/JSM, ENAR default |
| Portrait | 36 × 48 | 91 × 122 | AACR, ASCO default |
| Tabloid | 11 × 17 | 28 × 43 | Lab meetings, internal review |

## Recommended toolchain

**Quarto's built-in `format: typst`** is the lab's preferred poster
format. It produces print-quality PDFs faster than LaTeX, supports the
`--rl-*` token system naturally, and works well with R-rendered figures.
**No third-party extension required** — the starter template uses direct
Typst page-layout primitives (`#set page`, `#grid`) so it renders out of
the box on any Quarto 1.4+ install.

Alternatives that also work:

- **LaTeX `beamerposter`** — battle-tested for math-heavy posters; slower
  build, more verbose source. Use this if you need fine-grained control
  over equation typography.
- **LaTeX `tikzposter`** — modern LaTeX framework, good defaults.
- **PowerPoint** — only as a last resort. Hard to version-control, hard
  to keep aligned with the lab's typography system.

## Typography

| Role | Font | Use |
|------|------|-----|
| Poster title | Source Serif 4, 80–110pt | Top of poster |
| Author block | Archivo, 32–40pt | Below title |
| Section headings (h2) | Archivo, 36–44pt, weight 600 | Block labels |
| Body | Archivo, 24–28pt | Main content |
| Eyebrow / metadata | IBM Plex Mono, 18–22pt, uppercase | Section eyebrows |
| Figure captions | Source Serif 4 italic, 18–22pt | Below figures |

The 24–28pt body is the floor for readability at typical poster-viewing
distance (3–5 ft). At 36×48", anything below 22pt is unreadable.

## Color palette for posters

All web tokens carry over. Two adjustments specific to print:

- **Background**: white (`#FFFFFF`). Avoid the web's `--rl-fog` tint —
  it doesn't reproduce reliably across CMYK printers.
- **Hairlines**: bump to 1.5–2pt for print legibility (web uses 1px,
  which disappears under most print-shop drum scanners).

Otherwise the palette is identical to the web spec — see
[`colors.qmd`](colors.qmd).

## Layout grid

A clean academic poster reads in **3 column blocks** for landscape or
**2 column blocks** for portrait. Each block has a consistent eyebrow +
heading + content pattern.

```
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  [Logo]   POSTER TITLE — SERIF, 90PT                                 │
│           Authors · Affiliations · IBM Plex Mono caps                │
├──────────────┬───────────────────────────┬───────────────────────────┤
│ BACKGROUND   │ METHODS                   │ RESULTS                   │
│              │                           │                           │
│  body...     │  body...                  │  body...                  │
│              │                           │                           │
├──────────────┼───────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────┤
│ APPROACH     │ FIGURE                    │ CONCLUSIONS               │
│              │  ┌────────────────────┐   │                           │
│  body...     │  │   panel            │   │  body...                  │
│              │  └────────────────────┘   │                           │
│              │  Figure 1. caption        │  CONTACT · QR ···         │
└──────────────┴───────────────────────────┴───────────────────────────┘
```

## Quarto Typst starter

Create a new poster from
[`assets/branding/poster-template.qmd`](../assets/branding/poster-template.qmd):

```bash
cp ~/rashid-lab-setup/lab-handbook/assets/branding/poster-template.qmd ./my-poster.qmd
cp ~/rashid-lab-setup/lab-handbook/assets/branding/logos/RLlogoA.png ./
quarto render my-poster.qmd
```

The template uses Quarto's built-in Typst format. Render with:

```bash
quarto render my-poster.qmd
```

Typst is included with recent Quarto versions; no separate install needed.

::: {.callout-tip}
## Font installation

For the rendered PDF to use **Archivo + Source Serif 4 + IBM Plex Mono**
(the lab's brand fonts), install them on your system. On macOS / Linux,
the simplest path is to download from Google Fonts and drop the `.ttf`
files into `~/.fonts/` (Linux) or `~/Library/Fonts/` (macOS), then run
`fc-cache -f` (Linux only).

If the fonts aren't installed, Typst falls back gracefully to system
defaults — the layout still renders, but visual fidelity to the rest
of the lab system isn't preserved. Quarto will print warnings like
`unknown font family: archivo` to flag this.
:::

## YAML frontmatter

The starter template uses minimal YAML so Quarto's default article
wrapper doesn't fight the body's page-size `#set` rule:

```yaml
---
format:
  typst:
    keep-typ: true
keep-md: false
---
```

`keep-typ: true` is optional — it preserves the generated `.typ` file
alongside the PDF, useful for debugging layout issues.

All page setup, brand tokens, title block, and footer live in the
Typst code blocks below the YAML. See
[`assets/branding/poster-template.qmd`](../assets/branding/poster-template.qmd)
for the full source.

## Color & font setup in Typst

The starter template includes this block at the top of the body. Adapt
the page dimensions for portrait or different conferences as needed:

```typst
#set page(
  width: 48in,
  height: 36in,
  margin: (top: 1.0in, bottom: 0.8in, left: 1.0in, right: 1.0in),
  fill: white,
)

#let rl-navy          = rgb("#13294B")
#let rl-navy-deep     = rgb("#0B1D3A")
#let rl-carolina      = rgb("#4B9CD3")
#let rl-carolina-deep = rgb("#2E6A9F")
#let rl-graphite      = rgb("#5B6670")
#let rl-ink           = rgb("#1A202C")
#let rl-fog           = rgb("#F4F6F8")
#let rl-mist          = rgb("#E6EAEF")

#set text(font: "Archivo", fill: rl-ink, size: 26pt)
#show heading: set text(font: "Archivo", weight: "semibold", fill: rl-navy)
#show heading.where(level: 2): set text(size: 40pt)
#show heading.where(level: 3): set text(size: 30pt)
#show raw: set text(font: "IBM Plex Mono", size: 0.95em)
#show link: set text(fill: rl-carolina-deep)
#show emph: set text(font: "Source Serif 4", style: "italic")
```

## Print prep checklist

Before sending to a print shop:

- [ ] Convert all figures to **300 DPI minimum** (600 DPI for fine line
      art, schematics, gel images).
- [ ] Embed all fonts in the PDF (Quarto/Typst does this by default;
      LaTeX requires `pdftex` with embedded fonts).
- [ ] Check color profile: most print shops want **CMYK** for offset
      printing, **sRGB** for digital roll printing. Confirm.
- [ ] Add **3mm bleed** on each edge if the print shop requests it
      (many do for full-bleed layouts).
- [ ] Save the final PDF with **flattened layers** — some print shops
      reject layered PDFs.
- [ ] Order at least **2 days before the conference** for shipping
      buffer; 1 week if the conference is across the country.

## Logo placement

- Top-left or top-right of the poster, sized to ~10% of poster width.
- Use `RLlogoA.png` on white poster bg; `RLlogoAwhite.png` if you have
  a navy/Carolina header bar.
- For multi-affiliation posters, add UNC Gillings + UNC Lineberger logos
  in addition to the lab logo. Get those from
  [UNC's official brand site](https://brand.unc.edu/).

## Don'ts

These are the same anti-patterns the website's visual register avoids,
adapted for print:

- ❌ Decorative gradients on backgrounds or section headers
- ❌ Drop shadows or 3D effects on figures or text
- ❌ Heavy colored boxes around content blocks (white space + hairline
   rules carry the layout)
- ❌ Marketing-orange `#FF9E1B` (use the dulled `#C48A1A` if you need a
   warm accent)
- ❌ More than 3 colors total (navy + Carolina + one accent maximum)
- ❌ Comic Sans, Arial, or Times New Roman as the primary typefaces.
   Always use Archivo, Source Serif 4, IBM Plex Mono.

## Examples

The lab does not currently maintain a public gallery of past posters.
For reference, the visual register the new tokens produce closely
matches the [Stanford Statistics poster style guide](https://statistics.stanford.edu/)
and the [Berkeley Biostat poster examples](https://publichealth.berkeley.edu/research/) — restrained, typographic, hairline-driven.
